# Slice 2.5 — dispatch plan

Spec: [`spec.md`](./spec.md). Branch: `slice/one-differ-two-ir-planner` (off merged main `720677e43`). Grounding inventory: the scout report (2026-07-06) — key figures below are file:line-grounded from it.

Six units, sequential, one persistent implementer + one persistent reviewer. Every unit behaviour-neutral except W4 (the sanctioned tree-view cut). The legacy walk keeps running until W4 deletes it — new machinery lands **in parallel**, then consumers switch, then legacy dies (the slice-2 `compute*` pattern).

## W1 — Leaf nodes + required discriminants

**Outcome:** the six relational IR classes (`SqlColumnIR`, `SqlForeignKeyIR`, `SqlUniqueIR`, `SqlIndexIR`, `SqlCheckConstraintIR`, `PrimaryKey` — none implement `DiffableNode` today) implement `DiffableNode`: `id` derived from existing fields (column = name; PK = fixed sentinel; FK/unique/index = deterministic column-tuple key; check = name), `isEqualTo` comparing **own attributes only** (never children), `children() => []`. `PostgresTableSchemaNode.children()` yields columns/PK/FKs/uniques/indexes/checks **+ policies**. `kind`/`nodeKind` become **required** on `SqlSchemaIRNode` (A08) — the relational node-kind vocabulary lands here.
**Behaviour-neutral:** additive; the legacy walk doesn't consume `children()`. Fixtures clean.
**Completed when:** node unit tests pin id/isEqualTo/children per class; typecheck across all packages (the required-discriminant ripple the A08 attempt measured — ~90+ construction sites — is resolved properly, not cast around); `fixtures:check` clean.

## W2 — Resolution at derivation (parallel, unconsumed)

**Outcome:** both sides carry resolved values on the new leaf nodes. Expected: `contractNamespaceToSchemaIR`/`contractToPostgresDatabaseSchemaNode` stamp resolved native types (codec `expandNativeType`), rendered defaults (`postgresRenderDefault`), and resolved value-sets onto the leaves at contract→IR. Actual: introspection stamps normalized values (`parsePostgresDefault`/`normalizeSchemaNativeType`; SQLite equivalents) at IR construction. `isEqualTo` compares the resolved/normalized values. The legacy walk is untouched (it still reads raw fields + self-normalizes) — raw fields remain alongside until W4.
**Completed when:** derivation tests pin resolved leaves both sides; legacy schema-verify suites (~3.8k lines) still green untouched; fixtures clean.

## W3 — The port: one differ detects the relational drift set

**Outcome:** `diffSchemas` over the two new trees reproduces the full §1-inventory drift set (missing/extra/changed table, column type/nullability/default drift, PK/FK/unique/index/check semantics — incl. semantic satisfaction: unique-by-index, index-by-unique-constraint, FK referential-action compare, name-insensitive PK). Semantic-satisfaction rules live in the nodes' `isEqualTo`/id design, not in the differ. Strict gating + control-policy disposition become **reason/category post-diff filters** in the SQL family verify (`classifySqlVerifierIssueKind` re-keyed on node kind + reason). The SQL family `verifySchema` verdict derives from the filtered issue list (`control-instance.ts:735-751`'s `counts.fail` read dies). `check_removed` → `not-expected` per spec. Legacy walk still present; a parity test suite proves differ-vs-legacy verdict equality across the ported schema-verify scenarios.
**Completed when:** the ported scenario suites (rewritten from the ~3.8k-line schema-verify cluster, keyed on reason + node) are green; parity pinned; verdicts identical in all modes; runner post-apply unaffected.

## W4 — Cut the tree (the deliberate output change)

**Outcome:** the legacy walk (`verifySqlSchema`/`verifySqlSchemaTree`/verify-helpers/control-verify-emit) is **deleted**; `SchemaVerificationNode`, `root`/`counts`, the `verifyDatabaseSchema` tree-producer hook, and grafted-node machinery are deleted from framework-components + families + targets. `db verify` renders verdict + issues + unclaimed (human + `--json`); `combine-verify-results` reshapes (no root/counts); `unclaimed-elements` operates on issues only; **Mongo result-envelope rewrite** — `diffMongoSchemas` keeps its algorithm but returns issue-only output; `verifyMongoSchema` verdict from issues; `scope-verify-result.ts` issues-only. `type_metadata_missing` warn channel dies here (recorded).
**Behaviour:** verdicts/ops unchanged; the rendered output change is the spec's sanctioned one. CLI output tests (~1.1k lines) rewritten to the issue-based shape.
**Completed when:** grep-clean for `SchemaVerificationNode`/`root`/`counts`-on-verify; all verify verdict tests green; guards green; Mongo suites (1.4k-line `schema-verify.test.ts` rewritten) green.

## W5 — `plan(start, end)`

**Outcome:** framework `MigrationPlanner.plan` loses `keepDiffIssue` and all issue vocabulary (`control-migration-types.ts:371-437` reshaped); its **structural** diff→op path takes two schema IRs and maps node-typed issues to ops — both `issue-planner.ts` switches (Postgres 1004 lines; SQLite parallel) dispatch on node kind + reason, reading nodes + the derivation-stamped op-render payload, never coordinate strings or the contract. `verify-postgres-namespaces` emits node-typed issues. Callers derive both sides: `synthStrategy` derives expected via `contractToSchema`; the offline `migration plan` op derives **both** (today it derives only the from-side, `migration-plan.ts:93-149`). `ISSUE_KIND_ORDER` dependency ordering re-keyed on node kind + reason.

**Scope of the contract-free bar (operator decision, 2026-07-07 — spec §Decision 5 + Non-goals).** `plan()` **retains** the contract in this slice, solely to run the **codec-hook subsystem** — the type-operations hook (`CREATE EXTENSION`/`CREATE TYPE`, `storageTypePlanCallStrategy`) and the field-lifecycle hook planner (`planFieldEventOperations`, ADR 213). Those are codec-owned op contributions, not structural diffing, and stay untouched (their unification onto the one differ is a follow-on slice). So the Postgres grep-clean is scoped to **structural op-render reads only** (`buildColumnTypeSql`/`buildColumnDefaultSql`/`buildExpectedFormatType`/`toDdlColumn` reads relocated onto `opRender`); `partitionIssuesByControlPolicy`/`partitionCallsByControlPolicy` (control-policy disposition, applied post-mapping) and the two codec hooks **legitimately keep** their contract reads — SQLite reached the stronger "zero contract" bar only because it has no extensions/custom types. The **NOT-NULL-column temp-default backfill** (`notNullAddColumnCallStrategy` → `resolveIdentityValue`) is made byte-safe by **stamping the resolved value onto `opRender`** at derivation (strategy reads the node, output identical); operator-approved disable is the fallback only if stamping the deferred-DDL call is infeasible. The `missing_schema` namespace stitch stays `verifyPostgresNamespacePresence` (reads `existingSchemas` off the root — the introspected tree's namespace set ≠ the declared schema set), converted to a synthesized `postgres-namespace` not-found node issue.
**Also in W5 — aggregate operation naming (operator decision, 2026-07-07):** the aggregate's "strategies" are two different operations, not two styles of planning: fabricate a new migration from a state diff vs resolve a path through recorded migrations. Rename `synthStrategy`/`graphWalkStrategy` to say what they do (plan-from-diff vs resolve-recorded-path; final names implementer's call, no "strategy" in them), and make `planMigration`'s per-space selection read as explicit cases, not strategy fallback. The empty-graph case (extension space shipping zero migration packages — all-external spaces like Supabase auth/storage) stops invoking the planner entirely: construct the declared-state no-op result directly (zero ops, destination = head ref, same edge bookkeeping via `buildSynthMigrationEdge`). Synth was only reused there because disposition-suppression made its plan come out empty; the direct construction honors check-integrity's documented rule ("ships no migrations ⇒ no DDL") instead of depending on the space having nothing managed. Behaviour identical for every real flow; fixtures stay byte-identical.
**Completed when:** planner-op parity proven by the **suites** + a **golden diff of real `plan()` output vs the committed example ops** (NOT `fixtures:check` — it re-serializes hand-frozen example ops and gates only contract emission, blind to planner ops for **both** targets; see spec §Behaviour contract correction); `fixtures:check` still clean (contract emission stable); planner/issue-planner/strategy suites (both targets) ported and green; no issue vocabulary in `plan()`'s signature and the structural op path grep-clean of contract op-render reads; aggregate planner reads as explicit operations (no "strategy" vocabulary); the empty-graph case never calls the family planner.

## W6 — One issue type: delete the legacy vocabulary

**Outcome:** `BaseSchemaIssue`/`SchemaIssue`/`EnumValuesChangedIssue` (as a coordinate union), the `outcome` field, the two-list `SchemaDiff` split, and the unclaimed `tableName` residual read are deleted; `SchemaDiff` carries one node-typed list; framework exports shrink; Mongo's issue construction moves to the node-typed shape (coordinates carried on its own nodes). Sweep: `partitionIssuesByControlPolicy` resolver callbacks, disposition classifiers, synth predicate — single-branch.
**Completed when:** AC-1 grep-clean (`SchemaVerificationNode`, `verifySqlSchemaTree`, `BaseSchemaIssue`, `outcome`); full slice gate (AC-6) green.

## Sequencing & gates

`W1 → W2 → W3 → W4 → W5 → W6`, strictly. Per-unit gate: build, forced typecheck, `fixtures:check`, `lint:deps`/`lint:casts`, scoped `--filter` suites for touched packages, the four multi-space guards. Slice gate (W6): the full set incl. `check:upgrade-coverage --mode pr --prev $(git merge-base bot/main HEAD)` and all three suites. **This slice DOES touch the public framework export surface** — expect upgrade-coverage to demand real entries (deleted exports: `SchemaVerificationNode`, `SchemaIssue`, changed `plan()` SPI); record them via the `record-upgrade-instructions` skill, don't declare incidental.

## W7 — PR #921 review fixes

Full findings + evaluation: [`reviews/pr-921/synthesis.md`](./reviews/pr-921/synthesis.md) (Will's 26 comments + architect + principal-engineer passes). Meta-theme: cross-cutting concerns leaked onto the core types (schema-IR node + framework issue) — pull each back to its layer once (fix-the-class). Ordered:

1. **Unblock CI + vocabulary:** relock `lint:framework-vocabulary` threshold to 852 (our deletions improved it below the locked 906); add `schema` to the 2-sql forbidden vocabulary (it's a Postgres word — `resolvedReferencedSchema` → namespace).
2. **Retire `opRender`; render column DDL via the codec registry** — the anchor change. The column node carries a `CodecRef` (query-AST pattern, TML-2456/TML-2918); the op-builder resolves `codecLookup.forCodecRef` (contract-free, already on the control stack); delete `opRender` on `SqlColumnIR`/`SqlColumnDefaultIR`, the `renderColumnOps` derivation callback, `columnOpRenderOf`, and the threaded `native-type-expander`. Byte-identical (render logic unchanged; only when/how the codec is reached). Full design: [`design-codec-render-via-registry.md`](./design-codec-render-via-registry.md). (Standing direction: TML-2405, TML-2317.)
3. **Ownership by coordinate, not bare name** — `SchemaOwnership.declaresEntity` takes an entity coordinate (namespace-qualified); dissolves the `POSTGRES_TABLE_PATH_DEPTH` string-path "hack" (ask the coordinate, don't count path segments); add the same-bare-name-different-namespace orphan-drop test.
4. **Pull the rest of the leaked concerns off the core types:** `diffRole` off `SqlSchemaIRNode` → a `roleOf(nodeKind)` classification in the verdict/disposition layer (keyed on the legitimate `nodeKind`, not string-suffix matching — do NOT re-introduce the W3-F1 sin); `controlPolicy` off `SqlTableIR` → resolved from the contract at disposition; `REASON_LABEL`/`reasonMessage` off the framework issue → into the CLI verify formatter; the formatter stops reading the two-list shape.
5. **Naming sweep** (fix-the-class): `control-result-types`, `SchemaFindingLists`→issues, `schema-diff-verify`, `diffSchemaForVerdict`, and the `synth` leftovers (`buildSynthMigrationEdge`/`appSynthFailure`/the user-facing message) the rename missed. **Comment cull:** the process/jargon comments ("the flip", "issue node", "for verdict", over-long blocks).
6. **Hygiene:** DRY the `isEqualTo` `blindCast` into a per-kind predicate + shared `assertNode<T>`; move the fixtures doc out of `docs/reference`/`docs/README` (user-facing) to an internal/contributor location; fix the wrong `diff-database-schema.ts` src-import comment; record the Mongo nominal-`DiffableNode`-conformance follow-up; trim the stale `SchemaVerifier` doc; correct AC-3 wording (in-scope sub-claims met; literal `(start,end)` deferred).

**Completed when:** all of Will's comments addressed, F01 green, op-parity re-proven (suites + golden, not `fixtures:check`), full slice gate green, PR review threads answered.

## Known blast radius (from grounding)

~30 test files / ~11k+ lines pin the tree/counts/coordinate-issue shapes: sql-family schema-verify cluster (~3.8k), Mongo `schema-verify.test.ts` (1.4k), CLI `output.test.ts` (1.1k), `diff-database-schema.test.ts` (841), SQLite `issue-planner.test.ts` (638), plus aggregate/combine/unclaimed/synth/planner suites. Postgres `issue-planner.ts` has **no direct unit test** — its coverage is via planner/strategy integration tests; W5 adds direct coverage while porting.
